rulings.question: Base-case rebinding policy for the /create:* YAMLs
rulings.ruling: Facade-preserve all existing .opencode/skills/sk-doc/... literals during the monolith split; do not explicit-repoint command YAMLs in the base conversion.
rulings.rationale: Every inspected command family binds to root sk-doc paths for templates, references, or scripts, so root facade symlinks give the required zero-command-edit migration path while nested packets move underneath.

rulings.question: create_parent_skill_{auto,confirm}.yaml rebinding
rulings.ruling: Facade-preserve in the split commit; allow explicit-repoint only as a later isolated cleanup after old-path facades exist and pass the parent-skill command flow.
rulings.rationale: This is the self-hosting hazard: the parent-skill command consumes the parent_skill_* templates that would scaffold the new hub, plus root validation scripts. Moving templates before old-path facades exist breaks the command needed to scaffold or repair the hub.

rulings.question: create_sk_skill_{auto,confirm}.yaml rebinding
rulings.ruling: Facade-preserve; do not explicit-repoint during the hub conversion.
rulings.rationale: The skill command binds to the root skill template set and root validation/package scripts, so a facade keeps both regular skill creation and the new create-skill packet stable while internals move.

rulings.question: create_agent_{auto,confirm}.yaml rebinding
rulings.ruling: Facade-preserve; keep the root agent_template, reference, and script paths readable.
rulings.rationale: The agent command only needs stable root access to one template, shared standards references, and document validators; explicit repointing adds churn without architectural benefit.

rulings.question: create_folder_readme_{auto,confirm}.yaml rebinding
rulings.ruling: Facade-preserve both README and install-guide paths; fold install-guide under create-readme internally behind old root paths.
rulings.rationale: The same command family binds to root README templates and root install-guide templates, so folding install-guide without root facades would break an existing command surface.

rulings.question: create_feature_catalog_{auto,confirm}.yaml and create_testing_playbook_{auto,confirm}.yaml rebinding
rulings.ruling: Facade-preserve; route old root feature_catalog and testing_playbook template paths to their packet-local assets or shared validators.
rulings.rationale: Both command families bind to root creation references, shared standards, package templates, and validators; keeping facades avoids duplicated command edits across auto and confirm variants.

rulings.question: create_changelog_{auto,confirm}.yaml rebinding
rulings.ruling: Facade-preserve; do not explicit-repoint until the keep-or-fold decision for changelog is settled.
rulings.rationale: The changelog command binds to a single root changelog template path in comments, config, and execution instructions, so the safest migration is a stable facade regardless of whether changelog remains a packet or folds into shared/doc-quality.

rulings.question: Atomic ordering for self-hosting-safe migration
rulings.ruling: Order the conversion as: create nested packet/shared targets first; add root facade symlinks for every old sk-doc path consumed by /create YAMLs; verify /create:sk-skill-parent while YAMLs still point at old paths; only then optionally repoint create_parent_skill YAMLs in a separate change.
rulings.rationale: The parent command requires direct old-path access to hub templates, registry/router/description/graph metadata templates, and validators, so the old paths must remain readable before, during, and after moving internals.

risks: Self-hosting breakage if .opencode/skills/sk-doc/assets/skill/parent_skill_* moves before old-path facades exist: .opencode/commands/create/assets/create_parent_skill_auto.yaml:178-.opencode/commands/create/assets/create_parent_skill_auto.yaml:182

README/install-guide regression if install_guide_template is folded without preserving .opencode/skills/sk-doc/assets/readme/install_guide_template.md: .opencode/commands/create/assets/create_folder_readme_auto.yaml:898
Premature changelog repoint could encode the unresolved keep-or-fold decision into command assets: .opencode/commands/create/assets/create_changelog_auto.yaml:279
open: Exact nested destination paths for facaded assets must be fixed by the architecture-decision phase before any optional explicit-repoint; inspected YAMLs currently specify only old root sk-doc paths: .opencode/commands/create/assets/create_sk_skill_auto.yaml:190-.opencode/commands/create/assets/create_sk_skill_auto.yaml:217
